title: Local Networking, Name Resolution, and Secure Remote Access · Operations Recall
summary: Restore access to a host by testing local configuration, route, name resolution, listening sockets, and SSH trust in causal order. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a layered connectivity and SSH trust runbook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret interface addresses, routes, neighbors, and listening or established sockets as different network evidence.
Trace hosts files, resolver configuration, search domains, DNS queries, caches, and application lookup results.
Test local process, local socket, route, name resolution, transport, and application protocol with the smallest useful probe.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Interface Route Socket evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Interface Route Socket assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Host has 192.0.2.40/24, default route 192.0.2.1, DNS 192.0.2.53, and service port 443. Name lookup succeeds while the socket is absent; test layers and preserve SSH host-key trust.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Interface Route Socket has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Interface Route Socket precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Host has 192.0.2.40/24, default route 192.0.2.1, DNS 192.0.2.53, and service port 443. Name lookup succeeds while the socket is absent; test layers and preserve SSH host-key trust.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A configured IP address proves the application port is reachable.

options.text: Capture user, directory, environment, target state, and authoritative command behavior
Predict shell parsing, expansion, permissions, side effects, exit status, and safety boundary
Run the smallest simulated observation or reversible change and retain exact output
Verify changed state, failure path, rollback, and an accessible operations handoff

stimulus.title: Remote Copy Tunnel case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Host has 192.0.2.40/24, default route 192.0.2.1, DNS 192.0.2.53, and service port 443. Name lookup succeeds while the socket is absent; test layers and preserve SSH host-key trust.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: SSH port forwarding exposes only harmless local traffic by definition.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use scp, sftp, rsync, and SSH forwarding with explicit direction, permissions, resume, exposure, and verification. Artifact under review: a layered connectivity and SSH trust runbook.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Remote Copy Tunnel, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Remote Copy Tunnel: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Host has 192.0.2.40/24, default route 192.0.2.1, DNS 192.0.2.53, and service port 443. Name lookup succeeds while the socket is absent; test layers and preserve SSH host-key trust.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use scp, sftp, rsync, and SSH forwarding with explicit direction, permissions, resume, exposure, and verification.
